folio.label: 92v
folio.l: Sire Clerk of Oxenfor¶ , oure host sayde
ñe ride as stille and coy , as dootú a mayde
Were newe spoused , syttynge at the bord
This day ne herd I , of ©oure tonge a word
I trowe ©e studye , a bouten som Sophyme
But Salamon seitú , æat euäy thyngö hatú tyme
For goddis sake , as betú of betre cheere
It is no tyme , for to studyen heere
Telle vs som mery tale , be ©oure feye
For what man , that is entred , in a pleye
He nedes must , vn to the pley assent
But preechetú nat , as freres don in lent
To make vs , for oure olde synnes wepe
Ne that thy tale , make vs nat to slepe
Telle vs sum merimeri thyngö , of auentures
ñoure termes , ©oure coloures , and figures
Kepe hem in stoor , til so be , æ æ ©e endite
Heye stile , as whan æ men , to kynges wryte
Spekitú so pleyn , at this tyme , . we ©ow preye
That we moun vnderstonde , what that ©e seye
¢ This worthy Clerk , benygnely answered
Host q¶ he , I am vnder ©oure ©erde
ñe han of vs , as now , the gouänaunce
And therfore wold I do ©ow , obeisaunce
As fer as resou~ axetú , hardily
I wold ©ow telle a tale , whicú that I
Lerned at Padowe , of a worthy Clerk
As proued be his wordes , and his werk
He is now deed , and nayled in his cheste
I preye to god , so ©eue his soule reste
Fraunceys Petrak , the laureat poete
Hygút this Clerk , whos Retoryk swete
Enlumyned al Itaille , of Poetrye
As lynyan dide , of Philosophye
Or lawe , or other art particuler
But detú that wol nat , suffren vs , dwellen heer
But as it were , a twynkelynge of an eye
Hem bothe hatú slayn , and alle shuln we deye
But fortú to tellen , of this worthy man
That taugút me this tale , as I began
I seye , that first , he witú hisheyehis eye stile enditetú
Or he the xxxdetyxxxxdety , of his tale wrytetúdety42Written over an erasure.ODR
A ¸bleme , in the whiche , descryuetú he
Pomond and of Saluces , the contree
And spekitú of Appenym , the hilles heye
That ben the boundes , of westlumbardye
And of Mount vesulus , in speciale
Where as the Poo , out of a welle smalle
